stages.description: Canonical, ORDERED value-chain stages — the single source of truth for the profit-pool migration view. Replaces three scattered hard-codings (data-module.js STAGE_ORDER/STAGE_LABEL + the template's STAGE_COLOR CSS vars). A company points here via company.chain_stage. Optional for backward-compat: when absent, the view falls back to its built-in constants. Adding a new stage (e.g. cloud / 需求侧) is then a pure-data change — append an item here and tag companies with chain_stage; no code edit. Array order defines left→right paint order; the separate `order` field is an explicit tiebreaker so reordering never depends on array position alone.

chain_stage.description: Key into meta.stages[].key — which value-chain stage this company occupies in the profit-pool migration. Optional + fallback: when absent (or meta.stages absent), the view/Selector falls back to its built-in STAGE_OF map keyed by company id. Replacing STAGE_OF with this field is the migration target, but both coexist (chain_stage || STAGE_OF[id]) so existing data needs no edit.

ai_exposure.description: Coarse AI-attribution bucket for the company as a whole, used only as the LAST-RESORT fallback in AI-share derivation (see ADR-3 priority ladder). pure = ~all profit is AI-driven; primary = AI is the dominant driver; partial = meaningful-but-minority AI; peripheral = incidental AI exposure. Recommended-required for populated companies, but kept optional here for a non-breaking rollout (migrate first, then tighten). NOT a number — the actual weighting share comes from ai_profit_share / is_ai proxy; this only seeds a conservative default and never overrides a sourced number.

ai_profit_share.description: Hand-entered share of the company's NET INCOME attributable to AI, ∈[0,1]. Optional. This is the TOP priority input to the C-weighted pool (pool = Σ net_income × aiShare). It is a sourced ESTIMATE, not a raw fact reconcilable to a filing — so when present it MUST be accompanied by ai_share_source (provenance per project invariant). null/absent ⇒ Selector falls back to the is_ai revenue proxy, then to ai_exposure (see ADR-3). Never derived-and-stored; if you can derive it from segments, leave this null and let the Selector compute the proxy at read time.

ai_revenue_share.description: Hand-entered share of the company's REVENUE attributable to AI, ∈[0,1]. Optional, display/context only — NOT used as the C-weighting key (the pool weights profit, not revenue). When present MUST carry ai_share_source. Distinct from the is_ai segment proxy the Selector can compute itself; provide this only when you have a cleaner top-down number than segment summation (e.g. a company-stated AI revenue figure).

ai_share_source.description: Provenance for ai_profit_share / ai_revenue_share — mandatory whenever either is non-null (enforced in validate.py, not by schema, to stay non-breaking). Each entry carries url + data_status exactly like every other source; AI-attribution numbers are typically data_status 'estimate' or 'derived'. Omit when both shares are null.

quarters.description: Optional quarterly atoms (raw facts only). TTM is DERIVED in Selectors (算不存): TTM = latest complete FY + quarters reported after that FY-end − the year-ago matching quarters. Never store TTM here. period_end is a machine-readable ISO date (deliberately NOT the free-text path of years[].period_end), so the Selector can date-align quarters.

quote.description: Single-slice market snapshot (not a time series). Raw market facts only; PE/PS/FCF yield are derived in Selectors, never stored.

market_cap.description: Market capitalization in USD bn — same unit/口径 as years[].revenue / net_income. The only cross-currency-safe value; use this for all valuation multiples.

net_debt.description: Net debt in USD bn (same unit/口径 as market_cap). 净负债 = 有息债务(排除经营租赁) − (现金及等价物 + 短期投资). 负数 = 净现金。Optional raw fact; EV = market_cap + net_debt and EV/Sales are derived in Selectors, never stored.

price.description: Local-currency share price, for display/对账 reference only. Never multiply across currencies.

price_currency.description: ISO 4217 currency of `price` (e.g. JPY, USD). Display only.

valuation_caveat.description: Honest distortion flags driving derived-layer downgrade (e.g. investment-holding companies like SoftBank whose net margin / PE are skewed by investment gains).

period_end.description: Free-text, human-facing period label (e.g. '截至 2024-01-28' / '自然年 2024'). Display only — NEVER parsed for date math. Retained for backward-compat and rich display.

period_end_iso.description: Machine-readable fiscal-year-end date, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). Optional. Upgrades the profit-pool migration's year alignment from regex-scraping the free-text period_end (data-module.js _yearOf) to a reliable key — the Selector should prefer period_end_iso when present and only fall back to the regex over period_end. Mirrors quarters[].period_end's machine-date contract; does not replace the free-text period_end (which stays for display).

capex.description: Capital expenditures (raw, from cash-flow statement). Sign-neutral magnitude in meta.unit.

cfo.description: Net cash from operating activities (raw, from cash-flow statement) in meta.unit.

quarter.description: A single reported fiscal quarter — raw atoms only. Exists to power DERIVED TTM (净利率/PE 等 TTM 口径) in Selectors; TTM itself is never stored. Distortion (e.g. SoftBank quarterly net income carrying investment gains/losses) is handled in the derived layer via valuation_caveat, not here — such companies may simply omit quarters.

period_end.description: Quarter-end date, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). Machine-readable — the Selector uses this to pick the latest quarter and find its ~365-day-ago match. Do NOT use the free-text style of years[].period_end.

label.description: Human label for the quarter, e.g. 'Q1 FY2027' or 'CQ1 2026'. Display only; never parsed for date math (use period_end).

net_income.description: Quarterly net income in meta.unit (USD bn). null = reported-but-unknown / not yet captured. The atom TTM net income rolls from.

revenue.description: Optional quarterly revenue in meta.unit (USD bn). Enables TTM revenue / PS-TTM derivation.

sources.description: Provenance, mandatory per project invariant — each carries url + data_status.

stage.description: One value-chain stage in the canonical ordered list (meta.stages). Carries everything the three former hard-codings held: key (the join key from company.chain_stage), label (display), color (the CSS color the template previously held in a STAGE_COLOR var), and order (explicit integer ordering). Adding a stage = appending one of these + tagging companies; no code change.

key.description: Stable join key referenced by company.chain_stage (e.g. design/foundry/memory/equipment/invest/app, and future cloud/demand).

label.description: Human display name (e.g. 设计/代工/存储).

color.description: Display color for this stage (the value the template's STAGE_COLOR CSS var carried, e.g. '#7dd3fc' or a CSS color token). Pure presentation, but lives with the stage so a new stage needs no template edit.

order.description: Explicit left→right paint order. Authoritative tiebreaker so ordering never depends on array position alone.
